title: Pride and Prejudice
creator: Jane Austen
excerpt: A novel of wit, pride, and the slow bloom of understanding between two stubborn hearts.

creator: George Orwell
excerpt: A stark vision of surveillance and language bent to power — still unnervingly close.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
creator: Harper Lee
excerpt: Compassion and moral courage seen through the eyes of a child in the American South.

title: The Great Gatsby
creator: F. Scott Fitzgerald
excerpt: Jazz Age glamour and longing, written in prose that glitters and then breaks your heart.

title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
creator: Gabriel García Márquez
excerpt: A family saga where memory, myth, and rain fall together in luminous sentences.

title: Beloved
creator: Toni Morrison
excerpt: A haunting masterpiece on memory, motherhood, and the cost of freedom.

title: The Odyssey
creator: Homer
excerpt: The original journey home — epic, human, and endlessly retold.

title: Frankenstein
creator: Mary Shelley
excerpt: A Gothic tale that asks who the real monster is — and never stops asking.

title: Wuthering Heights
creator: Emily Brontë
excerpt: Wild moors, fierce love, and a passion that refuses to stay buried.

title: The Brothers Karamazov
creator: Fyodor Dostoevsky
excerpt: Faith, doubt, and family tragedy explored with ferocious intelligence.

title: Invisible Man
creator: Ralph Ellison
excerpt: A surreal, searing portrait of identity and invisibility in America.

title: Mrs. Dalloway
creator: Virginia Woolf
excerpt: A single day in London, rendered as a flowing interior symphony.

title: The Stranger
creator: Albert Camus
excerpt: Spare, sun-bright prose about indifference, justice, and the absurd.

title: The Name of the Rose
creator: Umberto Eco
excerpt: A medieval mystery that doubles as a love letter to books and signs.

title: The Little Prince
creator: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry
excerpt: A fable for adults about wonder, responsibility, and what we tame.

title: Norwegian Wood
creator: Haruki Murakami
excerpt: Nostalgic, melancholy coming-of-age set to Beatles records and rain.

title: The Remains of the Day
creator: Kazuo Ishiguro
excerpt: Understated and devastating — duty, regret, and what goes unspoken.

title: A Room of One's Own
creator: Virginia Woolf
excerpt: An elegant argument for creative freedom — still radical, still relevant.

title: The Old Man and the Sea
creator: Ernest Hemingway
excerpt: A parable of endurance told in clean, hard lines like carved bone.

title: Crime and Punishment
creator: Fyodor Dostoevsky
excerpt: Guilt as a living thing — psychology before psychology had a name.

title: The Handmaid's Tale
creator: Margaret Atwood
excerpt: A dystopia told in whispers — chilling because it feels possible.

title: Brave New World
creator: Aldous Huxley
excerpt: Pleasure as control — a future that chose comfort over freedom.

title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
creator: Oscar Wilde
excerpt: Vanity, morality, and a portrait that keeps the score.

title: Moby-Dick
creator: Herman Melville
excerpt: Obsession, the sea, and sentences vast enough to hold a whale.

title: Jane Eyre
creator: Charlotte Brontë
excerpt: A governess's fierce conscience and a romance that earns its fire.

title: The Catcher in the Rye
creator: J.D. Salinger
excerpt: Adolescent alienation in a voice so direct it still feels immediate.

title: Slaughterhouse-Five
creator: Kurt Vonnegut

title: The Bell Jar
creator: Sylvia Plath
excerpt: A precise, painful map of depression beneath a bright surface.

title: Don Quixote
creator: Miguel de Cervantes

title: The Master and Margarita
